authoritative_sources.title: 29 CFR 1926.20 - General safety and health provisions
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: OSHA is the primary U.S. federal construction-safety regulator. This standard defines accident-prevention programs and frequent inspections by competent persons that can block building-engineering coordination work.

authoritative_sources.title: After You Get a Permit
authoritative_sources.publisher: New York City Department of Buildings
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public permitting, inspection, or field-service guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NYC DOB is an official authority having jurisdiction and its permit-holder guidance describes approved-plan posting, permit duties, required inspections, signoff, and permit closeout steps.
used_for: permit closeout routing and closure boundaries

authoritative_sources.title: What to Expect During an Inspection - Buildings
authoritative_sources.publisher: New York City Department of Buildings
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public permitting, inspection, or field-service guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: An official building-department inspection guide that describes inspection failure handling, stop-work implications, and the open items that can hold up final certificate issuance.

authoritative_sources.title: Service contracts
authoritative_sources.source_class: Scheduling, subcontractor, or work-order operating standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GSA publishes official facilities-operations contract guidance for repairs, preventive maintenance, required contractor reports, safety, and building-system service boundaries in federal facilities operations.

authoritative_sources.title: GSA PBS Preventive Maintenance Guide
authoritative_sources.source_class: Scheduling, subcontractor, or work-order operating standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GSA's preventive-maintenance guide is an official facilities operations reference for planned maintenance cadence, inspection intervals, and building-system service evidence used in work-order coordination.

authoritative_sources.title: Building Commissioning: The Process
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: WBDG is a recognized public federal and industry building-guidance platform. This commissioning reference defines schedule integration, systems integration meetings, issues logs, functional testing, deficiency correction, and turnover coordination.

authoritative_sources.title: Commissioning Documents: Process, Contents, and Acceptance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: This recognized public commissioning reference defines commissioning plans, checklists, issues logs, systems manuals, training, and final reports that govern evidence completeness for building-system handoffs.

authoritative_sources.title: Roles and Responsibilities in the Commissioning Process
authoritative_sources.source_class: Scheduling, subcontractor, or work-order operating standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: This recognized public workflow reference clarifies owner, commissioning provider, contractor, subcontractor, controls, TAB, and supplier responsibilities, making it authoritative for ownership and handoff boundaries.

authoritative_sources.title: Construction Quality Management (CQM) Training
authoritative_sources.source_class: Scheduling, subcontractor, or work-order operating standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: USACE publishes official CQM training modules covering quality-management planning, post-award orientation, submittals, managing the work system, and RMS/QCS handling that directly inform coordination artifacts and blocker management.

workflow_stages.name: Dependency intake and prerequisite validation
workflow_stages.description: Normalize request scope, work order, affected asset or system, permit status, outage window, inspection state, and owner context before any routing or closure output is produced.
source_classes: Public permitting, inspection, or field-service guidance
Scheduling, subcontractor, or work-order operating standards

workflow_stages.name: Site-readiness, safety, permit, and outage screening
workflow_stages.description: Check whether site access, approved plans, permit status, outage approvals, pre-task safety documents, and work-area controls are present before building-system work is handed off.
source_classes: Public permitting, inspection, or field-service guidance
Scheduling, subcontractor, or work-order operating standards

workflow_stages.name: Cross-team building engineering coordination and ownership resolution
workflow_stages.description: Identify prior owner, next owner, duplicated ownership, schedule conflicts, and required artifact completeness across facilities engineering, superintendent, permitting, inspection, commissioning, controls, TAB, safety, and trade teams.
source_classes: Scheduling, subcontractor, or work-order operating standards

workflow_stages.name: Inspection, startup, deficiency, and closeout blocker handling
workflow_stages.description: Preserve correction notices, failed inspections, startup gaps, open issues logs, TAB exceptions, incomplete training, and turnover-document gaps as blockers until the correct owner and evidence set are explicit.
source_classes: Public permitting, inspection, or field-service guidance
Scheduling, subcontractor, or work-order operating standards

workflow_stages.name: Authority-boundary escalation and closure confirmation
workflow_stages.description: Escalate whenever the request drifts into licensed trade judgment, engineer-of-record decisions, code interpretation, permit approval, inspection signoff, or safety-critical signoff, and confirm closure only after destination-owner acceptance is explicit.
source_classes: Public permitting, inspection, or field-service guidance
Scheduling, subcontractor, or work-order operating standards

artifacts: work orders, service requests, shutdown requests, methods of procedure, and affected-system lists
issued permits, permit cards, approved plans, inspection requests, correction notices, and AHJ correspondence
look-ahead schedules, outage calendars, integrated project schedules, owner maps, and dependency trackers
site logs, pre-task plans, safety observations, incident notes, stop-work notices, and area-access releases
RFIs, submittals, design clarifications, startup forms, TAB reports, controls integration notes, point lists, and deficiency logs
change orders, as-built redlines, O&M manuals, training records, warranty logs, systems manuals, and closeout packages
systems_of_record: building automation or controls evidence repository

allowed: Classify building engineering coordination requests as in scope, out of scope, or blocked on missing permit, site, inspection, outage, schedule, safety, startup, or closeout prerequisites.
Request the minimum tenant facts, systems-of-record links, and authoritative records required to continue safely.
Track duplicated ownership, stale handoffs, and dependency failures across facilities, permitting, inspection, safety, commissioning, controls, and trade teams.
Produce handoff summaries, blocker logs, routing confirmations, and closure confirmations when evidence is complete.
escalate: Permit status, inspection requirements, safety conditions, outage approvals, startup readiness, testing evidence, or closeout evidence are missing or materially conflict across sources of record.
The request requires permit approval, inspection signoff, code interpretation, licensed trade judgment, engineer-of-record review, or safety signoff.
Public guidance, tenant policy, contract terms, or jurisdictional instructions materially conflict on the next step.
A stop-work condition, inspection failure, startup gap, unresolved deficiency, or turnover issue changes whether the work may proceed.
refuse: Authorize work to proceed without required permit, site-readiness, outage, safety, inspection, or startup prerequisites.
Approve permit signoff, inspection signoff, code compliance, licensed trade signoff, engineer-of-record decisions, or design interpretation.
Close a handoff or turnover package without destination-owner acceptance and required artifact completeness.

domain_failure_modes: A building-system handoff proceeds without permit, shutdown, access-window, or approved-plan evidence and the team arrives unable to start safely.
Inspection or startup preparation ownership is ambiguous across facilities engineering, superintendent, commissioning, controls, and trade teams so the work window is missed.
The lane marks work complete even though correction notices, TAB exceptions, training records, as-built updates, or turnover documents remain unresolved.
Schedule pressure causes the lane to imply permit approval, code interpretation, engineer-of-record judgment, or licensed trade signoff that it does not own.
A changed site-safety condition or stop-work notice is treated as routine scheduling noise and the wrong team proceeds with building-system work.
Tenant-specific CMMS, BAS, or PMIS routing is missing, so the lane guesses the next owner and drops evidence continuity.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Request that implies licensed trade or code-interpretation authority
Work is handed off without required context or artifacts
Multiple teams appear to own the same step
The lane is asked to absorb execution work instead of coordinating it
Ownership is ambiguous across multiple teams
A handoff is attempted without the required context

unresolved_ambiguities.reason: Public AHJ guidance is authoritative only for the named jurisdiction, so runtime must retrieve the tenant's actual jurisdiction and local permit workflow before issuing a definitive routing package.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Tenant-specific shutdown and occupied-building approval rules
unresolved_ambiguities.reason: Occupied-building outage governance, hot-work rules, and shutdown-approval chains vary by tenant and facility; the lane must request those controls rather than infer them.
